audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Thessalonians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for apostolic tenderness, non-flattery, shared lives, word received as Divine word, persecution parallels, longing. Variant/reception-history caution required for 2:14-16.

verses.restored: For you yourselves know, siblings, our entrance toward you, that it has not become empty.
But after suffering and being dishonored in Philippi, as you know, we became bold in our Divine to speak the good news of the Divine to you in much struggle.
For our appeal is not from error, nor from uncleanness, nor in deceit.
But as we have been approved by the Divine to be entrusted with the good news, so we speak, not as pleasing humans, but the Divine who tests our hearts.
For we never came with flattering speech, as you know, nor with a pretext for greed; the Divine is witness.
Nor were we seeking glory from humans, neither from you nor from others, though we could have been weighty as apostles of Messiah.
But we became gentle in your midst, as a nursing mother cherishes her own children.
So longing for you, we were pleased to share with you not only the good news of the Divine, but also our own souls/lives, because you became beloved to us.
For you remember, siblings, our labor and toil; working night and day so as not to burden any of you, we proclaimed to you the good news of the Divine.
You are witnesses, and the Divine, how devoutly and righteously/justly and blamelessly we became toward you who trust.
Just as you know how, as a father with his own children, we appealed to each one of you and encouraged and testified,
so that you walk worthily of the Divine, who calls you into his own reign and glory.

And because of this we also give thanks to the Divine unceasingly, that receiving from us a word of hearing of the Divine, you accepted it not as human word but, just as it truly is, word of the Divine, which also works in you who trust.

For you, siblings, became imitators of the assemblies of the Divine in Judea that are in Messiah Jesus, because you also suffered the same things from your own country-people as they did from certain Judean opponents,
the same hostile pattern connected in the text with violence against the Lord Jesus, the prophets, and us, and with resistance to Divine pleasure and human good,
hindering speech to the nations so that they may be saved/healed, thus filling up misalignment; but judgment has come upon that pattern to completion.
But we, siblings, having been separated from you for a short season, in face not in heart, were more abundantly eager with great desire to see your face.
Therefore we wanted to come to you—I Paul, both once and twice—and the adversarial one hindered us.
For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting? Is it not even you, before our Lord Jesus at his presence/arrival?
For you are our glory and joy.

safety_note: 1 Thessalonians 2:14-16 has a deeply dangerous reception history and must not be used as a timeless condemnation of Jewish people. The restored draft keeps the conflict located in a specific hostile pattern within an intra-Jewish/early Jesus-movement setting and flags the passage for second-pass textual and reception-history audit.
